articles.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
articles.summary: Google on Monday disclosed that it identified an unknown threat actor using a zero-day exploit that it said was likely developed with an artificial intelligence (AI) system, marking the first time the technology has been put to use in the wild in a malicious context for vulnerability discovery and exploit generation. articles.title: Armed drones leading cause of civilian death in Sudan war: UN rights chief
articles.summary: Drones caused more than 80 per cent of civilian deaths in Sudan’s war during the first four months of 2026, killing at least 880 people, the UN human rights chief said on Monday, warning that escalating drone warfare could push the conflict into an even deadlier phase.

articles.title: Philippine VP Sara Duterte impeached for a second time
articles.summary: If convicted in a Senate trial, which is uncertain, she will be disqualified from running for president.

articles.title: Fostering breakthrough AI innovation through customer-back engineering
articles.summary: Despite years of digitization, organizations capture less than one-third of the value expected from digital investments, according to McKinsey research. That’s because most big companies begin with technological capabilities and bolt applications onto them, rather than starting with customer needs and working backward to technology solutions. Not prioritizing the customer can create fragmented solutions; disjointed…

articles.title: cPanel CVE-2026-41940 Under Active Exploitation to Deploy Filemanager Backdoor
articles.summary: A threat actor named Mr_Rot13 has been attributed to the exploitation of a recently disclosed critical cPanel flaw to deploy a backdoor codenamed Filemanager on compromised environments. articles.title: Whales could be harmed by diverted ships avoiding Middle East, scientists warn
articles.summary: Ships have rerouted around the Cape of Good Hope since 2023 to avoid conflicts in the Middle East.

articles.title: Trump calls Iran response to US proposal to end war 'totally unacceptable'
articles.summary: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz de-escalation is urgent, says UN chief
articles.summary: As the Strait of Hormuz crisis deepens and tensions between Iran and the United States remain unresolved, oil prices rose again early Monday, prompting the UN Secretary-General to call for a peaceful resolution and warn of the widening fallout across Africa and beyond.

articles.title: Hubble Survey Sets Up Roman’s Future Look Near Milky Way’s Center
articles.summary: The Milky Way’s galactic bulge, the bulbous region that surrounds the galactic center, contains a dense collection of stars, planets, and other free-floating objects. This region has been studied for decades with numerous ground-based and space-based telescopes, including NASA’s Hubble and James Webb space telescopes. articles.title: NASA Astronaut Jessica Meir
articles.summary: NASA Astronaut Jessica Meir sits for a portrait at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston on Sept. 23, 2025. This photo was chosen as one of the 2025 NASA Photographer of the Year finalists. articles.title: Ollama Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability Allows Remote Process Memory Leak
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security vulnerability in Ollama that, if successfully exploited, could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to leak its entire process memory. articles.title: US passengers from ship quarantined as officials say public risk 'very low'
articles.summary: One passenger has tested positive for Andes virus, a rare type of hantavirus, while another is showing mild symptoms, health officials say.

articles.title: Portrait looted by Nazis found in home of Dutch SS leader's descendants
articles.summary: The painting is believed to have been plundered by high-ranking Nazi Hermann Goering during World War Two.

articles.title: Washington dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty
articles.summary: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, was charged with attempting to assassinate US President Donald Trump.

articles.title: Dozens of Nigerian fishermen feared dead after Chad air strikes on Boko Haram
articles.summary: A fishermen's leader tells the BBC that 40 people are feared dead - either in the strikes or drowning as they tried to flee.

articles.title: Ailing Iranian Nobel laureate given bail and hospital transfer
articles.summary: Iranian authorities granted human rights activist Narges Mohammadi bail amid growing concern over her poor health in prison.

articles.title: His father had just been buried. Then West Bank settlers forced him to dig up the body
articles.summary: The UN human rights office condemns incident as "'appalling and emblematic of the dehumanisation of Palestinians" in the West Bank.

articles.title: Police find body believed to be of fugitive Australian shooter
articles.summary: A body found in New South Wales is thought to be that of a man wanted over a triple murder.

articles.title: Ukraine: Over 3,000 attacks on healthcare since full-scale Russian invasion
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has verified more than 3,000 attacks on healthcare in Ukraine since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in February 2022, the UN agency reported on Friday.

articles.title: Fake OpenAI Privacy Filter Repo Hits #1 on Hugging Face, Draws 244K Downloads
articles.summary: A malicious Hugging Face repository managed to take a spot in the platform's trending list by impersonating OpenAI's Privacy Filter open-weight model to deliver a Rust-based information stealer to Windows users. articles.title: US and French nationals test positive for hantavirus after leaving ship
articles.summary: The American national has arrived in Nebraska, while a French woman is isolating in Paris.

articles.title: Central African Republic: Funding cuts put humanitarian support at risk
articles.summary: The Central African Republic (CAR) is making progress towards stability and security but major aid budget cuts threaten humanitarian operations there, a senior official with the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Friday in New York.

articles.title: NASA’s SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Mission Overview
articles.summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting a mid-May launch to deliver scientific investigations, supplies, and equipment to the International Space Station. Loaded with about 6,500 pounds of supplies, the SpaceX Dragon spacecraft will lift off aboard the company’s Falcon 9 rocket from Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida. articles.title: Innovation abounds in device charging
articles.summary: The changes may be less perceptible than in smartphones, tablets, or wearables, but chargers have also been quietly reinvented over the last decade. articles.title: Your Purple Team Isn't Purple — It's Just Red and Blue in the Same Room
articles.summary: Defending a network at 2 am looks a lot like this: an analyst copy-pasting a hash from a PDF into a SIEM query. A red team script is being rewritten by hand so the blue team can use it. A patch waiting on a change-approval window that's longer than the exploitation window itself. articles.title: Fake Call History Apps Stole Payments From Users After 7.3 Million Play Store Downloads
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered fraudulent apps on the official Google Play Store for Android that falsely claimed to offer access to call histories for any phone number, only to trick users into joining a subscription that provided fake data and incurred financial loss. articles.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
articles.summary: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero" infection. articles.title: Bosnia's powerful peace envoy quits, with questions over role's future
articles.summary: Christian Schmidt had been in the role since 2021, and departs after appearing to lose the backing of the United States.

articles.title: Electricity bills targeted in planned shakeup to energy pricing
articles.summary: The war in the Middle East has brought renewed attention to Britain's vulnerability to energy price shocks.

articles.title: Passengers leave hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife as WHO says outbreak ‘not another COVID’
articles.summary: Passengers and crew from the cruise ship MV Hondius began disembarking in Tenerife on Sunday under a tightly coordinated international health operation led by Spanish authorities and the World Health Organization (WHO), as officials sought to reassure the public that the outbreak “is not another COVID.”

articles.title: WHO chief heads to Tenerife to oversee Sunday arrival of hantavirus-hit ship
articles.summary: The Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO), Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, has issued a direct plea for calm and solidarity to the citizens of Tenerife ahead of the scheduled arrival of the MV Hondius on Sunday

articles.title: Nuclear energy in the Middle East: A realistic choice or a risk?
articles.summary: As global electricity demand grows, so does the popularity of nuclear energy. In the Middle East, several countries are evaluating or advancing nuclear power projects, balancing weighty issues such as regional security, climatic conditions and international cooperation.

articles.title: TCLBANKER Banking Trojan Targets Financial Platforms via WhatsApp and Outlook Worms
articles.summary: Threat hunters have flagged a previously undocumented Brazilian banking trojan dubbed TCLBANKER that's capable of targeting 59 banking, fintech, and cryptocurrency platforms. The activity is being tracked by Elastic Security Labs under the moniker REF3076. articles.summary: The dark secret of enterprise security operations is that defenders have quietly institutionalized the practice of not looking. articles.summary: One of the UK's largest chicken producers and a water company accused of polluting three rivers including the River Wye

articles.title: First Person: Surviving death row in Thailand
articles.summary: A Thai woman who spent more than 20 years in prison after being found guilty of drugs trafficking – including eight on death row – has told the UN how learning to sew helped her find meaning in life behind bars, and a job when she was released.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Haiti crisis continues, Gaza-West Bank update, Sir David Attenborough at 100
articles.summary: Haiti continues to face escalating violence and deepening insecurity, with gangs expanding their reach beyond Port-au-Prince and civilians increasingly caught between armed groups and security operations.

articles.title: ‘Victims need a voice, assistance and justice’: UN victims’ rights advocate calls for stronger action
articles.summary: The United Nations Victims’ Rights Advocate has called for stronger and sustained action on behalf of the people she serves across the UN system.

articles.title: Quasar Linux RAT Steals Developer Credentials for Software Supply Chain Compromise
articles.summary: A previously undocumented Linux implant codenamed Quasar Linux RAT (QLNX) is targeting developers' systems to establish a silent foothold as well as facilitate a broad range of post-compromise functionality, such as credential harvesting, keylogging, file manipulation, clipboard monitoring, and network tunneling.
